description: Cross-domain concordance linkage map — merged from two independent research passes. Extends coverage beyond XS01-XS10 syndromes.
merge_notes: v2.0 combines granular per-finding entries (compass research) with cross-organ chain formalization, stress discrimination logic, and source catalog (original research). 28 concordance entries + 5 cross-organ chains.

tier_1.label: Strongest
tier_1.definition: MI + concordant OM + concordant LB + dose-response
tier_1.example: Thyroid follicular hypertrophy + thyroid weight increase + T4 decreased + TSH increased + dose-response

tier_2.label: Strong
tier_2.example: Neuronal degeneration + clinical signs (tremors) + dose-response (no LB available)

tier_3.label: Supportive
tier_3.definition: 1 domain + dose-response + biological plausibility from class knowledge
tier_3.example: Follicular cell hypertrophy at high dose only, no OM or LB data collected

tier_4.label: Insufficient
tier_4.definition: Single marginal finding in one domain only
tier_4.example: Minimal gliosis at one dose without clinical signs, brain weight changes, or dose-response

BONE_MARROW_HYPOCELLULARITY.label: Bone Marrow Hypocellularity (General)

expected_om.notes: Bone marrow not weighed; spleen weight increase reflects compensatory EMH

expected_lb.notes: inappropriately low — nonregenerative; distinguishes production failure from peripheral destruction

concordance_logic.hypocellularity_without_cytopenias: acute onset (circulating cells from pre-treatment production) or sampling artifact
concordance_logic.cytopenias_without_bm_changes: peripheral destruction (hemolysis, consumption) rather than production failure — different mechanistic interpretation
concordance_logic.emh_with_anemia: supports regenerative capability; absence of EMH with severe anemia = concerning for marrow failure

BONE_MARROW_HYPOCELLULARITY.timing_notes: Neutropenia 1-14d; reticulocyte decrease 2-3d; thrombocytopenia 9-14d; anemia delayed (weeks due to RBC lifespan)
BONE_MARROW_HYPOCELLULARITY.diet_restriction_note: Diet restriction sufficient to stop weight gain caused 50%, 40%, and 20% decreases in erythroid, myeloid, and megakaryocytic precursors respectively; M:E ratio unaffected

expected_om.notes: Bone marrow not weighed; spleen may increase with compensatory myeloid EMH

expected_lb.notes: most sensitive indicator; temporal lag 2-3 days from BM
delayed onset due to RBC lifespan (~60 days rat, ~45 days mouse)
earliest and most sensitive indicator of erythroid production failure

BONE_MARROW_ERYTHROID_HYPERPLASIA.label: Bone Marrow Erythroid Hyperplasia (Regenerative)
BONE_MARROW_ERYTHROID_HYPERPLASIA.key_distinction: Effective hyperplasia: increased reticulocytes + stabilizing RBC. Ineffective: hyperplasia + continued anemia + inadequate reticulocytes = intramedullary precursor death

expected_lb.notes: onset 9-14 days (longest latency of any lineage)

SPLEEN_EMH.label: Splenic Extramedullary Hematopoiesis
SPLEEN_EMH.species_note: Normal background in rodents (mice > rats, females > males, young > old); compare to concurrent controls. In non-rodents (dogs, primates), always pathological.
SPLEEN_EMH.interpretation_note: EMH is compensatory, not primary toxic effect. EMH without marrow changes or cytopenias: consider primary splenic pathology or early/mild response

HEMOLYTIC_ANEMIA.label: Hemolytic Anemia Cascade

primary_lb.note: distinguishes hemolysis from production failure; 3-5 day lag
reticulocytes are larger than mature RBCs

HEMOLYTIC_ANEMIA.species_note: Haptoglobin reliable in dogs; confounded in rats (acute phase protein — rises with inflammation). Cats highly susceptible to oxidative hemolysis.

THYROID.label: Thyroid Disruption

expected_lb.notes: less reliably measured in rats; different protein-binding characteristics

LIVER_MEDIATED.regulatory_note: UGT-mediated thyroid tumors in rats accepted as not human-relevant by FDA, ECHA CLP, ESTP. Compound-specific MOA demonstration still required.
LIVER_MEDIATED.caveat: Not all enzyme inducers that decrease T4 increase TSH — 3-methylcholanthrene and PCBs decrease T4 but do not consistently elevate TSH or stimulate follicular cell proliferation (Hood 1999), suggesting T3 glucuronidation rather than T4 alone may mediate the TSH response

c_cell_hyperplasia.note: separate from follicular cell pathway; GLP-1 receptor agonist class effect in rodents

THYROID.species_note: Rats lack thyroid-binding globulin (TBG), relying on albumin for T4 transport; T4 half-life is 12-24 hours versus 5-9 days in humans. Rats uniquely sensitive to UGT-mediated T4 clearance.
THYROID.discordance_flag: thyroid_MI_with_normal_T4_TSH — either early/mild effect, measurement artifact, or non-TSH-mediated mechanism

lb_measurement_requirements.reason: diurnal variation in T4 and TSH

ADRENAL_CORTICAL_HYPERTROPHY.label: Adrenal Cortical Hypertrophy

stress_discrimination.rule: When adrenal cortical hypertrophy co-occurs with >=3 stress indicators OR the diagnostic triad, classify as stress-related (secondary) unless specific morphologic features indicate direct toxicity
stress_discrimination.thymus_sensitivity_note: Thymus is the most sensitive organ to glucocorticoid-mediated stress. If spleen/lymph nodes depleted but thymus preserved → suspect primary immunosuppression, NOT stress.

ADRENAL_CORTICAL_HYPERTROPHY.primary_vs_secondary: SECONDARY (stress): constellation of BW loss + thymus atrophy + adrenal hypertrophy + stress leukogram. Cannot distinguish from direct effect by histopath alone — both result from increased ACTH.
ADRENAL_CORTICAL_HYPERTROPHY.corticosterone_confound: Corticosterone measurement in rodents confounded by blood collection stress; dogs preferred for cortisol assessment

ADRENAL_CORTICAL_ATROPHY.label: Adrenal Cortical Atrophy (Tertiary — HPA Suppression)
ADRENAL_CORTICAL_ATROPHY.notes: Most commonly tertiary: exogenous glucocorticoid suppresses HPA axis → decreased ACTH → decreased trophic stimulation. Zona glomerulosa relatively preserved (ACTH-independent regulation via renin-angiotensin).

ADRENAL_CORTICAL_VACUOLATION.label: Adrenal Cortical Vacuolation (Primary — Steroidogenesis Block)
ADRENAL_CORTICAL_VACUOLATION.notes: Pathognomonic for steroidogenesis disruption. Key examples: ketoconazole (CYP11B1/CYP17A1 inhibition), aminoglutethimide, etomidate, mitotane (selective adrenolytic to zona fasciculata/reticularis). Distinguished from stress by ABSENCE of BW loss and stress markers.

ADRENAL_MEDULLARY_HYPERPLASIA.label: Adrenal Medullary Effects

expected_lb.notes: epinephrine/norepinephrine; rarely measured in standard studies

ADRENAL_MEDULLARY_HYPERPLASIA.species_note: Common age-related finding in rats (especially F344 males). Progression: hyperplasia → adenoma → carcinoma. Chronic stress increases incidence.

TESTES_DEGENERATION.label: Testicular Germ Cell / Tubular Toxicity

expected_om.notes: Insensitive to mild-moderate injury; >50% tubule loss needed for weight change. Germ cells lost through degeneration are replaced by intratubular fluid, maintaining volume.

expected_lb.notes: only with severe injury or direct Leydig cell toxicity
with severe germ cell loss — decreased inhibin B from Sertoli cells

TESTES_DEGENERATION.key_caveat: MOST testicular toxicity occurs with NORMAL circulating hormones. Direct germ cell and Sertoli cell toxicants produce tubular degeneration and oligospermia WITHOUT altering testosterone, LH, or FSH. Only Leydig cell damage or HPG axis disruption reliably changes hormones. Histopathology is the gold standard — more sensitive than weights, hormones, or fertility.
TESTES_DEGENERATION.sensitivity_hierarchy: MI > OM > LB for early detection
TESTES_DEGENERATION.stage_specificity: Stage VII/VIII of spermatogenic cycle in rat is most vulnerable to germ cell loss

stress.mechanism: GnRH suppression → decreased LH/FSH → decreased testosterone → stage VII/VIII degeneration
stress.discrimination: concurrent stress indicators (decreased BW >10%, increased adrenal weight, decreased thymus weight)

immaturity.note: sexually immature animals have incomplete spermatogenesis mimicking toxicity

TESTES_LEYDIG_CELL_CHANGES.label: Testicular Leydig Cell Effects

expected_om.notes: increased with large tumors or extensive hyperplasia; decreased with atrophy

expected_lb.notes: increased with functional tumors; decreased with atrophy

TESTES_LEYDIG_CELL_CHANGES.species_note: Focal/multifocal in rats, diffuse in mice. Rat Leydig cell tumor mechanisms may not translate to humans.

PROSTATE_SEMINAL_VESICLE_ATROPHY.label: Male Accessory Sex Organ Atrophy

expected_om.notes: Seminal vesicle weight is most sensitive androgen status indicator; ~40% decrease with androgen deprivation

PROSTATE_SEMINAL_VESICLE_ATROPHY.key_caveat: ASO weight changes can occur WITHOUT testicular weight changes. Stress/feed restriction → decreased GnRH → decreased testosterone → ASO atrophy but testes weight preserved. Always check testes first — if testicular findings present, ASO changes are secondary.

OVARY_FOLLICULAR_CHANGES.label: Ovarian Follicular Effects

expected_om.notes: Highly variable with estrous cycle stage (CV 15-40%). Not recommended for routine weighing (Sellers 2007).

expected_lb.notes: with follicular depletion — loss of inhibin feedback

OVARY_FOLLICULAR_CHANGES.required_concurrent_data: vaginal cytology for estrous cycle staging at necropsy
OVARY_FOLLICULAR_CHANGES.evaluation_approach: Dixon et al. 2014: two-tier — qualitative histopathologic assessment followed by quantitative follicle counting when triggered by concerns

UTERUS_ATROPHY.label: Uterine Atrophy

expected_om.notes: 2-3 fold variation with estrous cycle (CV 25-60%). Sellers 2007 does NOT recommend routine weighing.

cross_organ_link.finding: FOLLICULAR DEPLETION or DECREASED CORPORA LUTEA

UTERUS_ATROPHY.notes: Almost always secondary to ovarian/hormonal changes. Discordance with ovarian findings suggests exogenous hormonal effects or direct uterine toxicity.

CNS_NEURONAL_DEGENERATION.label: Central Nervous System Effects

expected_om.notes: Brain weight is highly conserved (CV 3-5%); changes rare but significant when present. Even 5-10% decrease warrants investigation. Most neurotoxic lesions do NOT produce detectable weight changes because they are focal/regional.

expected_lb_emerging.notes: neurofilament light chain; AUC 0.97-0.99 for detecting neural injury in plasma; EU IMI TransBioLine/NeuroDeRisk; not routine

CNS_NEURONAL_DEGENERATION.concordance_paradigm: CL_to_MI_primary. No routine LB biomarkers exist for CNS injury in standard studies. Clinical signs are the primary in-life indicator.
CNS_NEURONAL_DEGENERATION.functional_assessment: FOB or Irwin screen data should be cross-referenced with neuropathology

temporal_interpretation.transient_cl_signs: pharmacodynamic effect — may not have structural correlate
temporal_interpretation.progressive_cl_signs: structural damage likely — expanded neuropathology warranted

PNS_AXONAL_DEGENERATION.label: Peripheral Nervous System Effects

expected_lb_emerging.notes: sensitive for PNS and CNS; cannot distinguish site of origin

PNS_AXONAL_DEGENERATION.expanded_evaluation_trigger: chronic, slowly reversible or irreversible PNS clinical signs

SKIN_DERMAL.label: Dermal / Application Site Effects
SKIN_DERMAL.draize_note: Draize scores (0-4 for erythema and edema) may not always correlate with histopathology severity (Chandra 2015)

INJECTION_SITE.label: Injection Site Effects

route_considerations.subcutaneous: granuloma and fibrosis common; slower absorption

INJECTION_SITE.noael_note: Local reactions do NOT determine systemic NOAEL unless secondary systemic effects occur (stress from pain, infection)

LIVER_THYROID_AXIS.label: Hepatic Enzyme Induction → Thyroid Axis

check_sequence.mi: HYPERTROPHY, FOLLICULAR CELL; HYPERPLASIA, FOLLICULAR CELL; COLLOID DEPLETION

LIVER_THYROID_AXIS.partial_chain_note: thyroid MI without liver MI → suspect direct thyroid mechanism (TPO/NIS inhibition) → different regulatory implication (potentially human-relevant)
LIVER_THYROID_AXIS.species_relevance: Rat-specific mechanism. Rats lack TBG, T4 half-life 12-24h vs 5-9d in humans. NOT relevant to human risk per FDA, ECHA, ESTP.

BM_BLOOD_SPLEEN_AXIS.label: Bone Marrow → Blood → Spleen Compensatory Axis

sequence.lb: cytopenias — non-regenerative with inappropriately low reticulocytes

sequence.mechanism: RBC clearance + iron recycling + compensatory hematopoiesis

BM_BLOOD_SPLEEN_AXIS.emh_interpretation: Three processes induce EMH: (1) marrow damage, (2) myelostimulation exceeding marrow capacity, (3) abnormal circulating hematopoietic factors. EMH without marrow changes or cytopenias → investigate alternative mechanisms.
BM_BLOOD_SPLEEN_AXIS.species_note: Splenic EMH is normal background in rodents (mice > rats). In dogs/primates, always pathological.

STRESS_WASTING_CASCADE.label: Stress / Wasting Multi-Organ Cascade

pattern_check.flag_text: Findings at this dose level may reflect stress-mediated secondary effects — requires weight-of-evidence assessment

STRESS_WASTING_CASCADE.distinguishing_from_primary_immunotoxici: If spleen/lymph nodes depleted but thymus preserved → suspect primary immunosuppression, NOT stress. Thymus is most sensitive to glucocorticoid-mediated stress — if thymus sensitivity hierarchy is violated, stress is not the explanation.

TESTICULAR_REPRODUCTIVE_CASCADE.label: Testicular → Androgen-Dependent Organ Cascade
TESTICULAR_REPRODUCTIVE_CASCADE.rule: When prostate/epididymis/seminal vesicle weight decreases occur, check testes first — likely secondary to testicular toxicity rather than direct effects on accessory organs
TESTICULAR_REPRODUCTIVE_CASCADE.stress_pathway: Stress/feed restriction → decreased GnRH → decreased LH → decreased testosterone → ASO atrophy WITHOUT testicular weight changes (Halpern 2016). This pattern distinguishes non-specific stress from direct reproductive toxicity.

HEMOLYTIC_MULTI_ORGAN.label: Hemolytic Anemia → Multi-Organ Cascade

sequence.lb: RBC_decreased; HGB_decreased; HCT_decreased; reticulocytes_increased; bilirubin_indirect_increased; LDH_increased; haptoglobin_decreased

sequence.mi: HEMOSIDEROSIS; EXTRAMEDULLARY HEMATOPOIESIS; CONGESTION; MACROPHAGE HYPERPLASIA
sequence.mechanism: RBC clearance + iron recycling + compensatory hematopoiesis

sequence.mi: KUPFFER CELL PIGMENT; HEMOSIDEROSIS; KUPFFER CELL HYPERPLASIA
HEMOGLOBIN CASTS; TUBULAR HEMOSIDERIN; TUBULAR NECROSIS

HEMOLYTIC_MULTI_ORGAN.species_note: Haptoglobin reliable in dogs; confounded in rats (acute phase protein). Cats highly susceptible to oxidative hemolysis.
HEMOLYTIC_MULTI_ORGAN.note: Most extensive cross-organ chain in standard tox studies — spans 5 organ systems

cross_organ_elevation.rule: Cross-organ concordance elevates confidence by one tier
cross_organ_elevation.example: Isolated thyroid weight increase (tier_3) + liver hypertrophy + decreased T4 = tier_1

discordance_reduction.rule: Discordant findings within an organ system reduce confidence
discordance_reduction.example: Thyroid follicular hypertrophy + normal T4/TSH = discordant — should not auto-classify as adverse

absence_informative.rule: Absence of an expected concordant finding in another organ is as informative as its presence
examples: Thyroid follicular hypertrophy WITHOUT liver changes → direct thyroid mechanism, not enzyme induction
Adrenal hypertrophy WITHOUT stress triad → primary adrenal effect, not secondary stress
Splenic EMH WITHOUT cytopenias or marrow changes → investigate alternative mechanism

metabolic_visceral.paradigm: LB↔MI primary concordance; LB changes often precede or accompany MI

endocrine.paradigm: LB (hormones) ↔ MI primary concordance; cross-organ cascades common
endocrine.note: Hormone-dependent downstream effects require multi-organ checking

cl_driven.paradigm: CL → MI primary concordance; minimal or no routine LB correlates
cl_driven.note: Finding scored as 'not_applicable' for LB corroboration is the EXPECTED pattern, not an evidence gap

hematopoietic.paradigm: Unique — organ cannot be weighed but has richest LB correlate set through CBC

S33.content: STP/ASVCP BM Working Group: CBC + BM histology as evaluation foundation; 91% concordance with clinical hematotoxicity

S34.content: Thyroid MOA framework: enzyme induction → T4 clearance → TSH → follicular changes

S35.content: 6th ESTP Workshop: adversity considerations for thyroid FCHH

S36.content: Adrenal structure, function, mechanisms of toxicity

S37.content: Stress responses in tox studies: comprehensive 54-page organ-by-organ review

S39.content: Pathogenesis of male reproductive toxicity: target sites and cascades

S40.content: STP: Evaluation of testicular and epididymal toxicity

S44.content: Differentiating testicular toxicity from sexual immaturity

S45.content: Thyroid MOA framework for risk assessment

S46.content: Enhanced TH clearance from hepatic enzyme induction as endocrine MOA

S_Hood1999.content: PB and PCN increased TSH 65%/95%; 625%/1200% follicular proliferation increases

S_Bartsch2018.content: Rodent thyroid tumors from UGT induction not human-relevant

S_Elmore2006.content: Splenic EMH interpretation in rodents vs non-rodents

S_ChapinCreasy2012.content: Circulating reproductive hormones in tox studies; most testicular toxicity occurs with normal hormones

S_Dixon2014.content: STP/INHAND female reproductive pathology; two-tier ovarian evaluation

S_Halpern2016.content: Stress-mediated ASO atrophy without testicular weight changes

S_Sellers2007.content: Organ weight recommendations; brain highly conserved; ovary/uterus weighing not recommended
